title: H-audit: Inventory all cli.*.description keys with bilingual + example coverage matrix
description: Produce a coverage matrix listing every `cli.*.description` key with columns: en-present, zh-present, has-example, command-group. Output to session-folder artifact `_help-audit.md` for traceability. Identifies gap list consumed by TASK-002. Also identify any orphan keys (referenced in code but missing from one locale) and any 5-visible-command (`install`, `doctor`, `serve`, `uninstall`, `config`) descriptions that lack examples or mention deprecated flags removed in rc.15.

files.change: Coverage matrix table + gap list grouped by command (install/doctor/serve/uninstall/config)

implementation: Grep both locale files for `^  "cli\..*\.description"` to extract complete key set
Build matrix: key | en | zh-CN | has-example | command-group | rc15-stale (mentions removed flag)
Identify the 5 visible top-level commands and verify their root description is present + example-bearing
Confirm parity: every key in en MUST exist in zh-CN and vice versa — flag mismatches
Write matrix as markdown table to `_help-audit.md` with explicit gap list at the bottom

reference.pattern: Bilingual i18n catalogue with strict parity (rc.12 broad-gate-fabric-lang convention)
reference.examples: Existing keys at en.ts:103-108 (install) and zh-CN.ts mirror — use as the parity baseline

criteria: File `_help-audit.md` exists in session folder with a markdown table covering 100% of `cli.*.description` keys from BOTH locales
Every row has columns: key, en, zh-CN, has-example, command-group, rc15-stale
Bilingual parity check section at bottom lists ZERO unmatched keys (or explicitly enumerates mismatches as bugs)
5 visible commands (install/doctor/serve/uninstall/config) each have a row showing whether root description carries an example
Verify command: `wc -l .workflow/.lite-plan/rc17-polish-2026-05-15/_help-audit.md` returns >0; matrix is grep-able for `cli.install.description`, `cli.doctor.description`, etc.

rationale.chosen_approach: Run a read-only audit before any rewrite so TASK-002 has a concrete, reviewable gap list rather than guessing what needs example-injection
alternatives_considered: Skip audit and edit en.ts/zh-CN.ts directly while reading
Write audit + rewrite as one task
decision_factors: Bilingual parity bugs are easy to introduce silently — explicit audit table is the cheapest defense
Two-phase split (audit then rewrite) keeps each git commit scoped per memory/project_grill_deferred_items.md
rationale.tradeoffs: Two commits + one artifact file vs. one combined commit; the audit artifact has no runtime value but is invaluable for review

manual_checks: Open `_help-audit.md` and visually scan that all 5 visible commands appear
Confirm parity section has zero unmatched keys (or logs mismatches as defects to fix in TASK-002)
success_metrics: 100% of cli.*.description keys covered in matrix
Bilingual parity = 100% or all gaps explicitly enumerated
